title: Red Wine on Linen
stainChemistry: Red wine = anthocyanins + tannins. Linen is cellulose fiber that shrinks under heat AND bleeds dye easily AND warps under wet treatment when structured. Same tannin chemistry as wine on cotton (cold + Dawn + peroxide + vinegar), but linen-specific cautions apply: structured pieces (linen suits, lined dresses, tablecloths with stiffeners) lose shape; dyed linen bleeds; vintage linen is fragile. NO alkaline cleaners on tannin (universal rule). NO hot water (linen shrinks aggressively).
whyThisWorks: Standard tannin chemistry on simple flat linen. Cold water + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar handles fresh wine without setting tannin or shrinking linen. The honest fork: simple linen napkin / shirt = home protocol; structured linen = pro from minute one (shape change risk + dyed-linen bleed).
defaultAssumption: Simple flat linen (napkin, basic shirt, plain skirt) = home protocol. Structured / lined / trimmed / dry-clean-only = pro from minute one. Vintage linen = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only label = pro. Structured / lined / trimmed / vintage = pro. Simple flat linen = proceed.
Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK. Pulls anthocyanin out fast.
Dyed linen: hidden-seam test mandatory. Color transfer = pro lane. Clean = proceed. Skip for white linen.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn cold water on damp cloth. Press 5 min. Skip OxiClean — alkaline darkens anthocyanin permanently.
Hidden test on dyed linen first. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.
Vinegar rinse 5 min. Keeps anthocyanin red (light) instead of drifting blue-purple.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot (linen shrinks aggressively). Air-DRY FLAT — never tumble-dry until fully clear.

steps: 1. Care label + structure check. Structured = pro.
3. Hidden dye test on colored linen.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — linen shrinks under hot
warnings: NEVER hot water — linen shrinks aggressively
NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen
Hidden-area dye test mandatory on colored linen

homeSolutions.agent: Structured Linen Goes to Pro; Simple Linen Stays Home
homeSolutions.instruction: Linen has two cases. SIMPLE LINEN (napkin, basic shirt, plain skirt) = standard tannin home protocol. STRUCTURED LINEN (suits, lined dresses, tablecloths with stiffeners, anything dry-clean-only or with embroidery / trim) = pro from minute one. Wet treatment on structured linen risks shape change AND dyed linen bleeds easily.
Flip inside out. Run cold tap water through the BACK of the stain for 1–2 minutes. Most effective fast move.

homeSolutions.agent: Hidden Patch Test on Dyed Linen
homeSolutions.instruction: If colored, dab Dawn + cold water on a hidden seam, press 2 min. Color transfer = pro lane. Clean = proceed.
Dawn in cold water, soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse. NO OxiClean — alkaline darkens wine.
3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Hidden test on dyed linen FIRST.
1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water, 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps wine on the red (light) side.
Cool wash. AIR-DRY FLAT. NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks linen + sets wine.

steps: 1. Structured / lined / trimmed = pro. Simple linen = home.
2. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
4. Hidden dye test on colored linen.
5. Dawn + cold water + soft toothbrush, 5 min.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is structured, dye bleeds on hidden test, hot water / dryer was used previously.
warnings: NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen

neverDo: Never chlorine bleach on dyed linen
fiberSensitivities: Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water
Dyed linen bleeds — hidden test mandatory
Structured linen warps under wet treatment

materialWarnings: Dyed linen bleeds — hidden test always

escalation.when: Structured / lined / trimmed linen, dry-clean-only label, dyed linen failed hidden test, hot water / dryer used previously, vintage piece.
escalation.whatToTell: "Red wine on [simple / structured / dyed / vintage] linen, age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline. [Residual / structured concerns]."

contextQuestions: Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Vintage linen is fragile fiber-wise. Structured pieces need professional finishing after wet treatment. Dyed linen bleeds — pre-applied wet treatment may have already caused bleed; disclose.
